articles.title: npmx: A New npm Registry Package Browser
articles.description: A smooth, fast way to browse packages on the official npm registry. It’s certainly fast, smooth, and you see more info up front and center - check out the axios page for example. “We’re not replacing the npm registry, but instead providing an elevated developer experience through a fast, modern UI.”
articles.author: npmx

articles.title: 📊 The State of JS 2025 Survey Results
articles.description: The results of the annual survey are here, compiling the opinions of over 12,000 JavaScript developers into a flurry of charts in areas as diverse as language pain points, frontend framework choices, build tool usage, how much of their code is AI generated, and what non-JS/TS languages they use.
articles.author: Devographics

articles.title: ESLint v10.0.0 Released
articles.description: This long-awaited milestone completes the removal of the legacy eslintrc config system, introduces a new config lookup algorithm that starts from the linted file (great for monorepos), adds JSX reference tracking to fix scope analysis issues, and more.
articles.author: ESLint Team

articles.title: It’s About to Get a Lot Easier For Your JavaScript to Clean Up After Itself
articles.description: A fun technical exploration of Symbol.dispose and using, two new features that’ll ease many headaches around cleaning up after yourself: closing connections, freeing resources, etc. Just watch out for the Muppets…
articles.author: Mat Marquis

articles.title: ▶  Evan You on Vite, Rust and the Future of JS Tooling
articles.description: The Vue.js creator joined the Better Stack podcast to discuss his path from building Vite to founding VoidZero and developing a Rust-based JS toolchain.
articles.author: Evan You and Better Stack

articles.title: 🤖 Debugging React with AI: Can It Replace an Experienced Developer?
articles.description: Nadia rigged up an app laced with subtle bugs, unleashed Claude on it, and… watched it fail to impress.
articles.author: Nadia Makarevich

articles.title: Shovel.js: What If Your Server Were Just a Service Worker?
articles.description: A full-stack framework and meta-framework built around the Service Worker model, using Web APIs wherever possible to provide a consistent server surface across Node, Bun, and edge runtimes.
articles.author: Brian Kim

articles.title: VerifyFetch: Fetch Large Files with Resume and Verification Features
articles.description: Imagine fetch but with the ability to resume downloads, check you downloaded what you meant to download, and fail fast on any corruption. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Hamza Ezzaydia

articles.title: 🔐 OTPAuth: One-Time Password (HOTP/TOTP) Library
articles.description: Node, Deno, Bun and browser library to generate and validate TOTP and HOTP one-time passwords used in two-factor auth.
articles.author: Héctor Molinero Fernández

articles.title: 📺 Shaka Player 5.0: Library for Playing Adaptive Media
articles.description: Play formats like DASH and HLS in the browser sans plugins. Supports offline store & playback via IndexedDB. (Demos.)
articles.author: Shaka Project

articles.description: webpack shares its 2026 roadmap. Topics include support for a universal target to compile code to run on numerous runtimes, building TypeScript without loaders, CSS modules without plugins, and more.
VoidZero shares its January 2026 recap covering updates to Oxlint, Oxfmt, Vitest, and more.
Deno Deploy is now GA as a platform for easily deploying JS/TS apps to the Web. There's also a 45 minute video where Ryan Dahl shows it off.
🤯 Promethee provides UEFI bindings for JavaScript. Write a UEFI bootloader in JavaScript instead of C? Yep.
🤖 Transformers.js v4 Preview – Run ML models in the browser on top of a new WebGPU runtime.
Bun v1.3.9 – Run multiple package.json scripts concurrently/sequentially with --parallel/--sequential, faster Bun.markdown.react(), regexps get a SIMD boost, and more.
Ink 6.7 – Build rich terminal apps with React. v6.7 adds concurrent rendering and synchronized updates (less flicker!)
Ember 6.10 – Cleanups and modernization for the stable, battle-tested framework.

articles.title: The CSS Selection: 2026 Edition
articles.description: A huge deep dive into real-world usage of CSS, drawing on data from over 100,000 websites. It’s valuable analysis that looks at stylesheet size, overall feature usage, and CSS complexity (including a single page with 210,695 CSS rules!) If you’re curious about the adoption recent CSS additions are seeing, this is a must read.
articles.author: Bart Veneman

articles.title: How a 💔 Broken Heart Made a Page 100x Slower in Safari
articles.description: A single emoji brought a dashboard to its knees, dragging out load times to ten seconds. Finding it took a journey through red herrings, performance timelines, and a binary search. The sort of oddity any of us could run into.
articles.author: Allen Pike

articles.title: Interop 2025: A Year of Convergence
articles.description: Work on the Interop project, an initiative that brings together Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and others to identify the areas of the web platform where interoperability matters most to developers, has concluded its fourth year. Here's an overview of the 2025 efforts, including key features that saw convergence across browsers: Anchor Positioning, View Transitions, and the Navigation API.
articles.author: Nicole Sullivan (WebKit)

articles.title: Shades of Halftone
articles.description: A thorough technical tour of all things pixelation, dithering and halftone. Maxime digs into the mathematical implementation of using GLSL shaders to create such effects. I really dig the aesthetic (an increasingly popular one in modern web design generally) explored here.
articles.author: Maxime Heckel

articles.title: The Browser Hates Surprises
articles.description: There are techniques you can use to avoid page-loading jank and content from shifting around, even if repainting is still necessary. This post looks at some solutions.
articles.author: Durgesh Rajubhai Pawar

articles.title: ▶  What’s Missing from the Web Platform?
articles.description: A 50~ minute big-picture episode where Scott and Wes run through their wishlist for the web platform (better form controls, various CSS ideas, etc).
articles.author: Syntax podcast

articles.title: A Guide to Browser DevTools - The Network Monitor
articles.description: Provides a solid starting point for getting to grips with the Network Monitor in your workflow — a tool that allows us to see and inspect the requests a browser makes as a page loads.
articles.author: Michael Li

articles.title: A Polyfill for the HTML Switch Element
articles.description: Thomas shares a new polyfill designed to bring the native HTML switch element functionality to all modern browsers.
articles.author: Thomas Steiner

articles.title: How (& Why) to Stop Users from Selecting Text on Your Website
articles.description: You can stop text selection with the user-select CSS property. I didn’t know it was something that was in the spec, removed, and then added back again.
articles.author: Rachel Kaufman

articles.title: The Logo Soup Problem: And How to Solve It
articles.description: Many sites show company logos as social proof, but sizing and laying out logos responsively can be tricky. Luckily, you can now use React Logo Soup instead – here’s a demo. If React isn't your thing, they also explain the algorithm behind it.
articles.author: Rostislav Melkumyan

articles.title: Peek: A JavaScript Library for 'Smart Header' Behavior
articles.description: You can see it in action on the page. It’s “smart” in that it will differentiate between small insignificant scrolls and intentional ones. You can customize the thresholds and delays and it works with any framework. Repo here.
articles.author: Chad Pierce

articles.title: Invoker Buttons Polyfill: A Polyfill for the Newly Supported command and commandfor HTML Attributes
articles.description: Both features are now baseline newly available since December 2025, but if you want deeper support this script will help. There’s a writeup here.
articles.author: Keith Cirkel

articles.title: Shaka Player 5.0: Library for Playing Adaptive Media
articles.description: Plays formats like DASH and HLS in the browser sans plugins. It can also store and play media when offline using IndexedDB. (Demos.)
articles.author: Shaka Project

articles.title: broz: A Simple, Frameless Browser for Screenshots
articles.description: Potentially useful if, like me, you're sick of fidgeting around to get a clean screenshot of a site.
articles.author: Anthony Fu

articles.title: SVG Studio: A Browser-Based Tool for Working with SVGs
articles.description: Has quite a few features including optimization, debugging and fixing SVG rendering issues, cleaning up SVGs exported from design tools, and better understanding the underlying code.
articles.author: gavin atkinson

articles.title: Toon Text: A Web Tool to Generate Cartoon-Style Text
articles.description: Not for every project, but a unique tool that combines some fun fonts you can choose from with CSS effects. Includes a number of different ways to customize the look before copying the final code.
articles.author: Andy Clarke
